message:
chore(beads): track bd-drgkw "Unify ACFS runtime-context resolution across repo-local helpers"

New open bug capturing the remaining 52 test_changelog_export_status.sh
failures after today's strict-mode audit fixed array scoping and
services-setup TARGET_HOME precedence. Scope: duplicated runtime-
context resolution across status/changelog/export-config/continue/
dashboard/cheatsheet/info/support/onboard/acfs-update wrappers;
some sourced helpers fall back to /home/ubuntu instead of cached
caller home; wrapper dispatch/bin_dir handling is inconsistent.

Explicitly flags that the fix should be a deliberate shared-resolver
pass and not regress fixed-system-binary lookup to satisfy fake PATH
getent tests. Evidence log:
/tmp/acfs-changelog-export-status.1260313.log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -597,6 +597,7 @@ {"id":"bd-dkhc","title":"Security: restore checksum verification for Atuin installer","description":"install.sh currently installs Atuin via a direct curl of GitHub releases/latest/download/atuin-installer.sh without checksums verification (regression vs acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target). Update checksums.yaml + scripts/lib/security.sh to track the new URL (or revert to setup.atuin.sh) and ensure install.sh runs Atuin only via the verified-upstream wrapper; keep Atuin non-fatal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:06:12.681145Z","updated_at":"2025-12-23T18:12:07.774060Z","closed_at":"2025-12-23T18:12:07.774060Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in commit 05d84e3 (Dec 22). Verified: install.sh uses acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target for Atuin, checksums.yaml has setup.atuin.sh with valid SHA256 (verified against live URL).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-dktj","title":"Test security.sh (checksums, https)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:04:52.674376611Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T18:19:16.384464553Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T18:19:16.384467268Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dktj","depends_on_id":"bd-prqs","type":"parent","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:04:52.754198554Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-dmff","title":"Add headless browser auth flow explanation","description":"# Task: Add Headless Browser Auth Flow Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description When users run `claude` or `gh auth login` on the VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But there's no browser on the VPS! The tool prints a URL that must be opened on the user's laptop. This "device flow" is completely unexplained. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx Add explanation before the auth commands: \`\`\`tsx

Your VPS doesn't have a web browser, so authentication works a bit differently:

  1. Run the login command (like claude)
  2. The terminal will display a URL (or code)
  3. Copy that URL and paste it in your laptop's browser
  4. Complete the login in your browser
  5. Return to your terminal — it should confirm success
If you see "Opening browser..." but nothing happens, that's normal! Just copy the URL shown and open it manually.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the device flow clearly - [ ] Step-by-step numbered list - [ ] Addresses "nothing happened" confusion - [ ] Appears before the auth command list - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use Laptop icon to reinforce "use your laptop's browser" - This should feel like a helpful heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding a visual diagram showing the flow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T18:42:00.199364Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T19:27:20.959969Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T19:27:20.959969Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: AlertCard with Laptop icon explaining device auth flow with 5-step process and 'Opening browser...' confusion addressed at lines 179-197","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-drgkw","title":"Unify ACFS runtime-context resolution across repo-local helpers","description":"The strict-mode audit on 2026-04-19 left tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh at 216/268 passing after fixing array scoping and services-setup TARGET_HOME precedence. The remaining 52 failures cluster around duplicated runtime-context resolution in status/changelog/export-config/continue/dashboard/cheatsheet/info/support/onboard/acfs-update wrappers: repo-local helpers trust poisoned ACFS_HOME/TARGET_HOME or stale state, some sourced helpers fall back to /home/ubuntu instead of cached caller home, and wrapper dispatch/bin_dir handling is inconsistent. Fix should be a deliberate shared resolver/precedence pass, not weakening fixed-system-binary lookup to satisfy fake PATH getent tests. Evidence log: /tmp/acfs-changelog-export-status.1260313.log","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:09:54.181322460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:11:06.635979850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-dvt","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive unit coverage (no mocks) + E2E logging","description":"# Goal Establish real-data, no-mock unit coverage and complete E2E integration scripts with detailed, human-readable logging. # Why - Current tests are partial (installer E2E + web Playwright). No full unit coverage across core Bash libs or manifest tooling. - E2E logs exist but are not standardized or granular enough for fast diagnosis. # Success Criteria - Unit coverage exists for manifest parser/generator, selection resolver, contract/security/preflight libs with real fixtures (no mocks/fakes). - E2E scripts cover bootstrap + selection + resume flows with structured logs and artifacts. - Tests are deterministic, diffable, and runnable locally + in CI. # Notes - "No mocks/fakes" means: use real manifests, real generated scripts, real flags; avoid stubbing core behaviors. - Logging should be timestamped, sectioned, and include command echo + exit codes. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:13.758710Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:16:59.666200Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T22:16:59.666200Z","close_reason":"All 6 children completed: coverage matrix, parser/generator tests (132 tests), installer lib tests (15 tests), test harness, selection E2E (26 tests), web E2E runner script.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa","tests"]} {"id":"bd-dvt.1","title":"Define coverage matrix + real fixtures catalog","description":"# Scope Create a concrete coverage matrix that maps each core component to a test type, and standardize a catalog of **real** fixtures used by unit tests. # Deliverables - Coverage matrix table (component → test type → owner → status). - Fixture catalog list (real manifest YAMLs, generated scripts, seed state files) with storage location and usage notes. # Detailed comments - Components to include: `packages/manifest` (schema/parser/generator), `scripts/lib` (install_helpers, contract, security, preflight, logging), `install.sh` selection/introspection, `tests/vm` E2E. - Fixtures must be real (valid YAMLs and generated outputs), not synthetic mocks. - Ensure fixtures are minimal but realistic; avoid brittle snapshots. # Acceptance criteria - Matrix is stored in repo (likely `docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md`). - Fixture catalog references actual files committed in repo. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:21.951298Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:46:31.078442Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:46:31.078442Z","close_reason":"Added docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md and docs/tests/fixtures_catalog.md with real-file coverage matrix + fixture catalog","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dvt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-dvt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:21.953283Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-dvt.2","title":"Unit tests: manifest parser/generator using real fixtures","description":"# Scope Add unit tests for `packages/manifest` (schema, parser, dependency resolution, generator outputs) using real YAML fixtures. # Detailed comments - Use real `acfs.manifest.yaml` plus a curated set of small, valid fixtures (no mocks). - Cover schema validation (new fields), dependency closure, cycle detection, and deterministic module ordering. - Validate generated `manifest_index.sh` content matches fixture expectations (IDs, phases, deps, defaults). # Acceptance criteria - Tests run via `bun test` (or existing harness) with real fixtures. - Failures are actionable (clear assertions and output). ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:28.350421Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:58:31.870363Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:58:31.870363Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive unit tests: schema.test.ts, parser.test.ts, generate.test.ts - 132 tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["manifest","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dvt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-dvt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:28.352603Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dvt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-dvt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:26:03.792033Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
